rulings.Case Title: GREG MORRIS VS. SAFECO INSURANCE CO. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 28, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA, THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA LAWYER ASSISTANCE, THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA BOARD OF GOVERNORS AMENDED DEMURRER TO FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Continued to August 5, 2009. Defendant must provide the Court with courtesy copies pursuant to San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE LABORERS HEALTH et al VS. JOHN NORTHORP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief To Set Aside Void Order Of Default And Void Default Judgments
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 28, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT JOHN NORTHORP Motion For Relief To Set Aside Void Order Of Default And Void Default Judgments. Motion is denied. Amended proofs of service indicate defendant was properly served with summons and complaint and had actual notice of the proceedings. Copies of additional and subsequent filing were sent to defendant's address, and none were returned undeliverable. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: TERRY G MORGAN VS. SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 28, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMWENT, OFFICER D. FONG # 1552, OFFICER GIONANNELLI # 977 Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint. Transferred to Department 301 to be heard the same day. Judge Woolard recuses herself. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARK STEWART VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA/CHP et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 28, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for negligence. Defendant is a public entity that cannot be liable for common law claims. See Miklosy vs. Regents of the University of California (2008) 44 Cal. 4th 876, 899. Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second cause of action regarding FEHA. Plaintiff must plead facts showing the existence of an employment relationship. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGIOU STUDIO, INC. VS. TELEKENEX, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 28, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT TELEKENEX, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Off Calendar, as moot. First Amended Complaint filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KENDRA TOMCIK DR et al VS. SIDNEY FERNALD DR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 28, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT SIDNEY FERNALD MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Motion to quash granted only for failure to indicate on the summons that defendant Fernald was served as an individual. (CCP section 412.30). Remainder of summons substantially complies with statutory requirements. Defendant Fernald is subject to the specific jurisdiction of California. He has purposefully availed himself of the forum benefits and the controversy is related to and arises out of defendant's contacts with the forum. Defendant agreed to provide services and referral in San Francisco, and conducted personal on site training in the plaintiff's San Francisco Office. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE D MURRAY VS. ADA CARTER

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 28, 2009, line 6, PETITIONER LAWRENCE MURRAY Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment Thereon. Granted. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)
